title: sempervivum penninum
intro: Sempervivum penninum, also known as Houseleek or Woolly Houseleek, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. Traditionally, Sempervivum penninum has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sempervivum penninum include wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support, as well as potential benefits for digestive issues and respiratory problems. Sempervivum penninum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
